name: waydroid-launch-commands
description: Working Waydroid launch commands for testing Android apps on this machine
type: feedback
---

To run Waydroid on this machine, use these exact commands:

```bash
weston -Swayland-weston --renderer=pixman &
WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-weston waydroid show-full-ui
```

Then install and launch APKs:
```bash
sudo sh -c 'cat /path/to/app.apk | waydroid shell -- sh -c "cat > /data/local/tmp/app.apk"'
sudo waydroid shell -- pm install /data/local/tmp/app.apk
WAYL

name: dropbear-build-requirements
description: How to build the bundled Dropbear SSH binary for Android - critical constraints and patches
type: project
---

Bundled SSH binary (libssh.so) is Dropbear 2024.86 cross-compiled with NDK r27.

**Why:** Gniza uses rsync over SSH for backups; the bundled Dropbear acts as the SSH transport.

**How to apply:** When rebuilding or debugging the SSH binary, these constraints are critical:

1. **Must be dynamically linked** — static-pie executables segfault
- **NEVER deploy to production servers** — never SSH into production servers or run install scripts. Only commit and push.
- [CSS debugging](feedback_css_debugging.md) — Never make blind CSS changes; inspect DevTools first, debug root cause, test before deploying
- [Test server (cPanel LXC)](reference_test_server.md) — LXC container "cpanel" at 10.0.3.10, approved for deployment

name: feedback-css-debugging
description: Stop making blind CSS changes - debug root cause first, test before deploying
type: feedback
---

When CSS isn't rendering correctly, STOP making blind changes and deploying repeatedly. Instead:
1. Inspect the actual rendered CSS in the browser (use DevTools screenshots from user)
2. Understand WHY a class isn't applying before changing it
3. Test locally or verify the built CSS contains the expected rules before deploying
4. After 2 failed attempts, sto

name: test-server-cpanel
description: LXC container "cpanel" at 10.0.3.10 is the test cPanel server for gniza4cp deployment
type: reference

Test server for gniza4cp is an LXC container named `cpanel`.

**Access:** `ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 root@10.0.3.10` or `/home/shuki/projects/lxc/ssh.sh cpanel`

**Deploy steps:**
1. SSH in and `cd /root/gniza4cp && git pull`
2. Run `bash scripts/install.sh`

**Why:** User approved this as a test server for deployment (2026-03-13). The "never deploy" rul
Linux backup manager — standalone or centralized backup server. Bash core + Python Textual TUI + Flask web dashboard.

- **Core scripts**: `lib/` (library functions), `bin/` (entry points)
- **TUI**: `tui/` — Python/Textual terminal UI
- **Web**: `web/` — Flask + DaisyUI + Alpine.js dashboard, port 2323
- **Config**: INI-style `.conf` files
- **Tests**: `tests/`
- **Install/deploy**: `scripts/`

- `gniza.conf` — global settings (including global RETENTION_COUNT)
- `targets.d/*.conf` — sources (what to back up)
- `remotes.d/*.conf` — destinations (where to store)
- `schedules.d/*.conf` — cron schedules (including per-schedule RETENTION_COUNT)

- rsync `--link-dest` for incremental hardlink dedup
- Atomic snapshots via `.partial` rename
- Source types: local, ssh, s3, gdrive
- Destination types: ssh, local, s3, gdrive
- MySQL/MariaDB dump support
- Retention: per-schedule override → global default (moved from targets/remotes to schedules)
- `flock`-based concurrency control
- Deploy script: `scripts/deploy.sh "message"` — commits, pushes, syncs to install dir, restarts web service
- Always deploy after making changes (don't wait for user to ask)

- Root: app `/usr/local/gniza`, config `/etc/gniza/`, logs `/var/log/gniza/`
- User: app `~/.local/share/gniza`, config `~/.config/gniza/`, logs `~/.local/state/gniza/log/`
- Gitea: `git.linux-hosting.co.il/shukivaknin/gniza4linux`

name: Bar track contrast
description: Use bg-gray-200 not bg-gray-100 for progress bar tracks — lighter backgrounds disappear into white section cards
type: feedback
---

Progress bar tracks must use `bg-gray-200 dark:bg-gray-700`, not `bg-gray-100 dark:bg-white/5`. The lighter variant is invisible against Filament section backgrounds. Also keep labels inside bars (not as separate headings above) — it's more space-efficient and keeps the data directly in context.

**Why:** Deployed a version wit

name: Always use Context7 before writing API code
description: Must consult Context7 MCP for library/API docs before writing any integration code
type: feedback
---

ALWAYS use Context7 MCP to check documentation before writing any code that interacts with a third-party API or library. Never assume API endpoints, parameters, or behavior from memory.

**Why:** Multiple times wrote code against Stalwart APIs that didn't exist or had different parameter names in the actual version (e.g., POST /api/

name: Filament action patterns
description: When to use FormAction vs wire:click vs mountAction in Filament v5
type: feedback
---

FormAction CAN perform side effects — the problem was never FormAction itself.

**Correct patterns:**
- `wire:click="methodName"` — simple direct Livewire calls (remove, save, toggle)
- `wire:click="mountAction('actionName')"` — trigger Filament actions with modals, confirmations, forms
- `FormAction::make()->action(...)` — works inside schema Actions component for s

name: Use Gitea for issues, not GitHub
description: Always create issues on Gitea, never on GitHub
type: feedback
---

Never use GitHub to create issues. Always use Gitea instead.

**Why:** User preference — Gitea is the primary development platform, GitHub is a mirror.

**How to apply:** When creating issues, PRDs, or any tickets, use the Gitea API or web interface, not `gh issue create`.

name: No co-author line in commits
description: Do not add Co-Authored-By lines to git commit messages
type: feedback
---

Do not add the `Co-Authored-By: Claude ...` line to git commit messages.

**Why:** User finds it unnecessary and doesn't want it.

**How to apply:** When creating git commits, omit the Co-Authored-By trailer entirely.

name: feedback_no_custom_css
description: NEVER use custom CSS or inline styles — always use Tailwind utility classes
type: feedback
---

NEVER use custom CSS (`style=` attributes, `<style>` blocks, or custom CSS classes) in Blade templates. Always use Tailwind utility classes exclusively.

**Why:** The user considers inline styles and custom CSS unacceptable in this project. Filament compiles standard Tailwind utilities so they work fine. The only exception is dynamic values like `width` for pr

name: Never comment on time or suggest stopping
description: Do not make comments about time of day or suggest saving work for later — just do the work
type: feedback
---

Never say "it's getting late" or suggest saving things for "next session." Just do the work. The user decides when to stop.

**Why:** User found it presumptuous and unhelpful. They want work done, not time management advice.

**How to apply:** Never reference time of day, session length, or suggest stopping. If asked "should I

name: Response style
description: Keep answers short and human-like, no bullet point lists with dashes
type: feedback
---

Answer shortly and naturally like a person. Don't use bullet point lists with dashes all the time. No human writes that way.

**Why:** User finds the AI-style verbose bullet-pointed summaries annoying and unnatural.

**How to apply:** Just say what was done in a sentence or two. Skip the formatted recap lists. Write like you'd write in a Slack message or a GitHub comment. Us

name: Short GitHub issue replies
description: Keep GitHub issue comments short, conversational, no dashes or bullet lists
type: feedback
---

Keep GitHub issue replies short. One or two sentences max. No technical deep-dives or config examples unless the user specifically needs a workaround. Never use dashes or bullet points. Write like a human.

**Why:** User prefers brief, natural responses on GitHub issues.

**How to apply:** When commenting on issues, state what was fixed in plain sentences.

name: Check before replying to tickets
description: NEVER post GitHub/Gitea issue comments without explicit user approval — always show draft first
type: feedback
---

NEVER comment on GitHub or Gitea issues directly. Always:
1. Show the user what I plan to reply
2. Wait for explicit approval before posting

This is a hard rule — no exceptions, even if the fix seems obvious.

**Why:** User wants full control over public-facing responses. Got burned by a premature comment posted without approval.

name: Ticket instructions
description: Always include clear actionable steps when commenting on GitHub issues
type: feedback
---

When commenting on GitHub issues, always include the exact commands or steps the user needs to run. Don't assume they know how to do it.

**Why:** External contributors shouldn't have to guess what to do. They need clear instructions.

**How to apply:** If a fix requires running a command, include the full command. If it requires a UI action, spell it out. Don't just

name: Always verify after changes
description: Must check that the panel and all affected services still work after every deploy or server-side change
type: feedback
---

Always verify the panel and affected services are accessible end-to-end after every deploy or server-side change. Don't declare something done without confirming it works from the user's perspective.

**Why:** User got frustrated multiple times when changes broke things and I didn't catch it before moving on.

**How to apply:**

name: Architecture changes (2026-03-26 to 2026-03-28)
description: FrankenPHP panel independence, PowerDNS replaces BIND9, Restic replaces tar/rsync, service layer extraction
type: project
---

Major architectural changes made in this session:

**FrankenPHP panel independence (port 2223)**
- Panel runs on its own FrankenPHP server, independent of nginx
- Self-signed SSL by default, certbot upgrades if hostname resolves
- Certbot deploy hook at `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks/deploy/jabali-panel.

name: project_feature_backlog
description: Planned features from GitHub issues that are on the backlog
type: project
---

- **#5 — Granular backup restore**: Restore individual sites, mailboxes, or databases from backups instead of full restore only. Use case: one hacked site among 10, or a user who accidentally deleted emails.
- **#33 — Mail disclaimer messages**: Per-domain email disclaimers appended to outbound mail (for law firms, medical, etc). Approach: altermime + Postfix content filter + per-domain templates in DB + admin UI toggle/editor.

name: Next session priorities
description: Current project state, pending work, and TODO list
type: project
---

Session completed: Granular backup restore (service + tests + UI), jabali update CLI with infra upgrades, install.sh hardening (silent failures, re-install detection, .env backup, home dir safety), removed deb package, docs update.

### Admin Domains Page
- ~~Add Settings dropdown per domain~~ DONE
- Add domain status tags to user Domains page (Phase 2 of ghost domain plan)
- Reply to GitHub issue #64 after verifying the feature works

### Webmail
- #63 Standardize webmail to mail.domain.ext (serve directly, not redirect)

### Migration
- Test cPanel/WHM migration with Stalwart email provisioning end-to-end
- Add `email.stalwart_import_maildir` agent command for Maildir import

### Infrastructure
- Test infrastructure setup

name: Use YARA-X not legacy YARA
description: User explicitly wants YARA-X (Rust-based next-gen) instead of legacy YARA/yara-python for malware scanning
type: feedback
---

Use YARA-X (`pip install yara-x`, `import yara_x`) not legacy YARA (`yara-python`).

**Why:** User explicitly requested YARA-X. It's the next-gen rewrite by VirusTotal in Rust — faster, stricter regex validation, better error messages.

**How to apply:** Import as `yara_x`, use `yara_x.Compiler()` for multi-source compilation

name: Isolation architecture
description: PHP-FPM isolation is handled by jabali-isolator (systemd-nspawn), not jabali-security
type: project
---

PHP-FPM isolation is managed by jabali-isolator using systemd-nspawn containers. Jabali Security has NO role in PHP hardening, open_basedir, disable_functions, or pool config management.

**Why:** Separation of concerns — Security only monitors (file scanning, process monitoring, brute-force detection, WAF events). Isolation is a separate system (jaba

name: Jabali Security project context
description: Standalone Linux security suite for shared hosting — ClamAV-free, event-driven, Python daemon with YARA-X, comprehensive CLI, REST API. Panel-agnostic.
type: project
---

Jabali Security is a standalone, panel-agnostic security suite for Linux shared hosting — an Imunify360 alternative.

**Why:** Designed as a general-purpose Linux security daemon first. Jabali panel integration is a future goal, but the suite must work independently on any Linu

name: Gitea SSH alias for git push
description: Use 'gitea:' SSH alias (not full hostname) for git remote — routes through proxy
type: reference
---

Git remote must use the SSH alias `gitea:` not the full `ssh://git@git.linux-hosting.co.il:2222/` URL.

The SSH config (`~/.ssh/config`) defines `Host gitea` with a ProxyCommand that routes through a local SOCKS proxy at 127.0.0.1:9689.

Example: `git remote set-url origin gitea:shukivaknin/jabali-security.git`
